content: Curiosity-driven 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agents can gate their own long-term memory compression process by treating uncompressed raw memory entries as "unlabeled data" for active selection, only compressing and retaining memory chunks that align with the agent’s current intrinsic curiosity signal. This creates a self-reinforcing loop where high-relevance exploration experiences are prioritized for long-term retention while low-value transient data is discarded without wasteful compression overhead.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
